INTRO PREVIEW‎

The bedroom was dimly lit, the soft glow of the bedside lamp casting warm, golden hues across the sheets. Outside, the city’s hum was distant, muffled by the thick curtains Mydei had drawn earlier, ensuring that nothing disturbed you.

You lay nestled against him, your body warm, soft, changing — and he loved it. Every part of it.

His arm was wrapped securely around your waist, his fingers splayed over the gentle swell of your stomach. His touch was reverent, careful, as if he was afraid he might disturb you — but in truth, he just couldn’t stop touching you.

“You’re beautiful,” he murmured, voice low, rough with something unspoken. His lips brushed against your temple, lingering there for just a moment before trailing down to your cheek.

He had always found you stunning, always admired you with a quiet intensity that few others ever got to see. But now? Now, carrying his child, glowing with the warmth of new life?

You were breathtaking.
